title: Peppery Crispy Brazilian Soybeans Stir-fry
description: A dairy-free-friendly post-workout dish featuring crispy soya beans flavored with a spicy kick and a peppery punch to cleanse the palate.
instructions: Place the soybeans in a pot, cover with water, and pressure-cook according to your appliance's instructions. Let it cool slightly.
Finely chop the chili peppers, onion, garlic, and ginger. Set aside.
In a wok or large skillet, heat sesame oil over medium-high heat.
Add chopped onion, then add garlic and ginger after 1 minute of cooking onions. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Toss in the chili peppers and cook for another minute to release their flavors.
Drain the cooked soybeans into the wok with its soaking water, adding it all to the mixture.
Mix in the soy sauce, rice vinegar, salt, and pepper. Stir everything together until well combined.
Cook for an additional 3-5 minutes until the soybeans are crisp and golden brown. Remove from heat.
Sprinkle sesame seeds on top for garnish if desired.
Serve hot as a snack or light meal.
